executiveSummary: NVIDIA GTC 2026 closes with a clear theme: ‘AI factories’ are becoming a first-class product, spanning rack-scale platforms (Vera Rubin), new validated designs, and cloud rollouts measured in millions of GPUs. Microsoft used the week to position Microsoft Foundry as the enterprise control plane for agentic and physical AI: Foundry Agent Service is now GA, Nemotron open models are landing in Foundry, and Azure is rolling out Vera Rubin NVL72 systems while extending sovereign AI options via Azure Local + RTX PRO Blackwell. On the policy front, the U.S. federal push to centralize AI governance continues, while OpenAI’s DoD classified-network deployment underscores accelerating government adoption of frontier models.

items.headline: Microsoft expands Foundry at GTC: GA Agent Service + Nemotron models + deeper Fabric/Omniverse integration
items.summary: Microsoft announced expanded Microsoft Foundry capabilities to build, deploy and operate production-ready AI agents on NVIDIA accelerators and open NVIDIA Nemotron models, alongside deeper integration with Microsoft Fabric and NVIDIA Omniverse for physical AI workflows.

items.headline: Foundry Agent Service is generally available (GA), moving Microsoft’s agent stack from preview to production
items.summary: Microsoft says the next-generation Foundry Agent Service and observability in the Foundry control plane are now generally available, with voice live API integration available in public preview.

items.headline: Azure becomes first hyperscale cloud to power NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 systems (rollout over coming months)
items.summary: Microsoft says Azure is the first hyperscale cloud to power on next-generation NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 systems, targeting inference-heavy, reasoning-based workloads across its modern liquid-cooled data centers.

items.headline: Azure Local expands sovereign AI options with RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition support
items.summary: NVIDIA and Microsoft highlight Azure Local support for the NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPU to run models on premises with Foundry Local services, targeting regulated and sovereign environments.

items.headline: AWS will begin deploying more than 1 million NVIDIA GPUs across global regions starting this year
items.summary: NVIDIA says AWS will deploy more than 1 million NVIDIA GPUs starting this year, spanning Blackwell and Rubin architectures, RTX PRO Blackwell Server Edition GPUs, and Groq 3 LPUs as part of an expanded infrastructure partnership.

items.headline: NVIDIA Cloud Partners double ‘AI factory’ footprint to more than 1 million GPUs and 1.7GW capacity
items.summary: NVIDIA reports its cloud partners have cumulatively deployed more than 1 million GPUs across AI factories, representing over 1.7 gigawatts of AI capacity and supporting sovereign AI deployments across multiple regions.

items.headline: NVIDIA announces an open Physical AI Data Factory Blueprint (GitHub release planned for April)
items.summary: NVIDIA introduced an open reference architecture that automates data generation, augmentation and evaluation for physical AI systems (robotics, vision AI agents, autonomous vehicles), with an open GitHub release planned for April.

items.headline: NVIDIA: Azure has deployed ‘hundreds of thousands’ of liquid-cooled Grace Blackwell GPUs in under a year
items.summary: NVIDIA says Microsoft has deployed hundreds of thousands of liquid-cooled NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GPUs across Azure’s global data centers in less than a year, supporting inference-heavy agentic AI workloads.

items.headline: Google ships a major upgrade to Gemini 3 Deep Think and opens early-access via the Gemini API
items.summary: Google announced a major upgrade to its specialized reasoning mode, Gemini 3 Deep Think, available in the Gemini app for Google AI Ultra subscribers and via early access for select researchers, engineers and enterprises through the Gemini API.

items.headline: Gemini 3 Deep Think highlights: 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 and 3455 Codeforces Elo (reported by Google)
items.summary: Google reports the upgraded Deep Think reaching 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 (verified by the ARC Prize Foundation) and an Elo of 3455 on Codeforces, plus 48.4% on Humanity’s Last Exam (without tools).

items.headline: ‘Physical AI’ becomes an enterprise category: NVIDIA blueprint ties together Cosmos models, orchestration and evaluation
items.summary: NVIDIA’s Physical AI Data Factory Blueprint describes modular workflows (curate/search, augment/multiply, evaluate/validate) built around Cosmos tooling and orchestration to scale data pipelines for robotics and vision AI.

items.headline: Microsoft is positioned as a physical AI platform partner: Azure is listed as a cloud provider for NVIDIA’s blueprint
items.summary: NVIDIA’s announcement names Microsoft Azure among the cloud providers for the Physical AI Data Factory Blueprint, and notes integration with an open physical AI toolchain on GitHub including Microsoft Fabric, Real-Time Intelligence and Microsoft Foundry.

items.headline: OpenAI finalizes a deal to deploy its models on U.S. DoD classified cloud networks
items.summary: Reuters reports OpenAI finalized a partnership with the U.S. Department of Defense to implement its AI models on secure cloud systems for a classified network, underscoring accelerating government adoption.

items.headline: U.S. federal ‘preemption’ push: Commerce evaluation of state AI laws is a key near-term milestone
items.summary: A legal analysis notes the U.S. Executive Order 14365 directs the Commerce Secretary to evaluate state AI laws, aiming to reduce a ‘patchwork’ of state rules and move toward a centralized national framework.

conversationStarters.title: Foundry Agent Service is GA — what’s your first production agent use case?
conversationStarters.talking_point: Microsoft says Foundry Agent Service is now generally available, which is a good moment to pick one workflow (IT helpdesk triage, sales research, procurement intake, finance close) and define the tools, data boundaries and success metrics needed to move from pilot to production.

conversationStarters.title: Do you have an ‘AI factory’ plan (capacity + governance), or just an LLM plan?
conversationStarters.talking_point: GTC messaging is shifting from ‘which model?’ to ‘what factory will run and govern agents at scale.’ That’s a natural on-ramp to discuss Azure capacity planning, observability, data residency and sovereign AI deployment patterns.

conversationStarters.title: Where should physical AI run: cloud, edge, or on-prem sovereign stacks?
conversationStarters.talking_point: With Azure Local + RTX PRO Blackwell support and NVIDIA’s physical AI blueprint, customers can mix cloud-scale simulation/training with on-prem inference for latency, privacy, or regulatory needs — worth mapping to each line of business.

conceptOfTheDay.tagline: A productized stack for turning compute + data into tokens at industrial scale
conceptOfTheDay.explanation: An AI factory is an end-to-end, production-oriented system that continuously turns data into model outputs (tokens) for many applications — combining accelerated compute, networking/storage, orchestration, model serving, observability and governance. The key shift is treating AI as a repeatable manufacturing process, not a one-off model deployment.
conceptOfTheDay.whyItMatters: For Azure/AI sellers, ‘AI factory’ language is a helpful way to move customer conversations from isolated proofs-of-concept to scalable programs: capacity commitments, platform choices (Foundry/Fabric), security controls, and where workloads must run (cloud vs. Azure Local for sovereign/regulatory constraints).
conceptOfTheDay.analogy: If a single model endpoint is a food truck, an AI factory is the entire restaurant chain: kitchen equipment (GPUs), supply chain (data pipelines), recipes (prompts/agents), health inspections (governance), and dashboards (observability) — designed to serve thousands of meals a day reliably.
keyNumbers: AWS plans to begin deploying more than 1 million NVIDIA GPUs starting this year (NVIDIA)
NVIDIA says cloud partners cumulatively deployed more than 1 million GPUs across AI factories (NVIDIA)
Azure deployed ‘hundreds of thousands’ of liquid-cooled Grace Blackwell GPUs in less than a year (NVIDIA)

videosPicks.title: NVIDIA GTC Keynote 2026
videosPicks.description: Full keynote from Jensen Huang covering agentic AI, AI factories, and physical AI, plus major platform announcements.

videosPicks.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026 Keynote | Key Moments in 12 Minutes
videosPicks.description: A short recap of the biggest moments and announcements from the keynote.

videosPicks.title: Learn Foundry Agent Service — Build, Host & Scale AI Agents (GA)
videosPicks.description: A focused overview of Microsoft Foundry Agent Service and what GA means for production deployments.

videosPicks.title: The NVIDIA Rubin Platform: Six New Chips, One AI Supercomputer
videosPicks.description: NVIDIA-produced explainer on the Rubin platform components and the shift to rack-scale compute units.

quickFacts.fact: AWS plans to begin deploying more than 1 million NVIDIA GPUs starting this year.
NVIDIA says cloud partners have cumulatively deployed more than 1 million GPUs in AI factories, representing over 1.7 gigawatts of AI capacity.
Google reports Gemini 3 Deep Think reached 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 and a 3455 Elo on Codeforces.

glossary.definition: NVIDIA’s rack-scale system configuration for the Vera Rubin platform, positioned as a unit of compute for ‘AI factories’ and large-scale inference/training deployments. Microsoft says Azure is powering on Vera Rubin NVL72 systems.

Microsoft’s platform for building, deploying and operating AI solutions on Azure, bringing together models, tools, data, observability and governance in a single control plane; Microsoft highlighted expanded Foundry capabilities at NVIDIA GTC.
A managed service in Microsoft Foundry for building and operating production-ready AI agents; Microsoft says it is generally available (GA) as part of its GTC announcements.

Deploying AI systems where data residency, operational control, and regulatory requirements are met within a customer-controlled or country-specific environment; NVIDIA and Microsoft frame Azure Local + RTX PRO Blackwell support as an enabler for sovereign deployments.
